assertions.expectation: Proposes a phased approach: control plane first, then CPU pools, then T4 dev, then inference (A100), then training (H100) last
assertions.reason: Response clearly outlines a 3-phase approach: Phase 1 (CPU + T4 dev nodes), Phase 2 (A100 inference), Phase 3 (H100 training), with control plane upgrade explicitly shown first in Phase 1

assertions.expectation: Control plane can be upgraded independently without affecting running workloads
assertions.reason: Response explicitly shows control plane upgrade first with command 'gcloud container clusters upgrade ml-platform-cluster --master' before any node upgrades

assertions.expectation: CPU service pools upgraded first as lowest risk — use standard surge settings (maxSurge=2-3)
assertions.reason: While CPU pools are upgraded first, the surge settings show maxSurge=10 and maxUnavailable=5, which are much higher than the expected standard settings of maxSurge=2-3

assertions.expectation: T4 dev pool upgraded next — dev workloads are tolerant of interruption
assertions.reason: T4 dev pools are upgraded in Phase 1 after CPU pools, with explicit coordination with dev teams for maintenance window and acknowledgment that dev workloads can tolerate interruption

assertions.expectation: A100 inference pool upgraded with careful surge settings (maxSurge=1) and PDBs to maintain serving capacity
assertions.reason: A100 upgrade shows maxSurge=3, maxUnavailable=1 (close to expected), and explicitly includes PodDisruptionBudgets configuration with minAvailable: 70%

assertions.expectation: H100 training pool upgraded last, during a scheduled gap between training runs — with cordon-and-wait or maxSurge=0/maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: H100 pools are upgraded last in Phase 3, uses a blue-green approach with new node pool creation and gradual migration, effectively achieving maxSurge=0 approach with zero interruption

assertions.expectation: Notes the 20-node parallelism limit — at 5,000 nodes, the full upgrade will take weeks and must span multiple maintenance windows
assertions.reason: Response does not mention the 20-node parallelism limit that GKE imposes on upgrades, though it does span 15 days across multiple maintenance windows

assertions.expectation: Mentions GPU driver compatibility testing in staging before each GPU pool upgrade
assertions.reason: Response does not mention GPU driver compatibility testing in staging environment before upgrading GPU pools

assertions.expectation: Addresses GPUDirect/RDMA networking validation after upgrading the H100 pool
assertions.reason: Response does not mention GPUDirect/RDMA networking validation specific to H100 pool upgrades
